---
title: "Knowledge Graph Integrity Audit"
subtitle: "The Cartographer's Audit — Agent Almanac Repository"
author: "Opaque Team (4 Shapeshifters)"
date: "2026-03-06"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    self-contained: true
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 5
params:
  findings_file: "findings.yml"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(yaml)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

findings <- yaml::read_yaml(params$findings_file)
```

## Executive Summary

This report documents the results of a knowledge graph integrity audit
of the [agent-almanac](https://github.com/pjt222/agent-almanac) repository,
conducted by an opaque team of 4 shapeshifter agents using the adaptive
coordination pattern.

The repository contains **`r findings$registry$skills` skills**,
**`r findings$registry$agents` agents**,
**`r findings$registry$teams` teams**, and
**`r findings$registry$guides` guides** connected by a web of
cross-references. This audit assessed the integrity of those connections.

```{r summary-table}
summary_df <- data.frame(
  Category = c(
    "Broken References",
    "Orphan Nodes",
    "Non-Reciprocal Cross-References",
    "Registry Integrity"
  ),
  Findings = c(
    findings$summary$broken_refs,
    findings$summary$orphan_nodes,
    findings$summary$non_reciprocal,
    findings$summary$registry_issues
  ),
  Severity = c("High", "Medium", "Low", "Info"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(summary_df, align = c("l", "r", "c")) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
    full_width = FALSE
  ) |>
  row_spec(
    which(summary_df$Severity == "High"),
    background = "#fce4ec"
  ) |>
  row_spec(
    which(summary_df$Severity == "Medium"),
    background = "#fff8e1"
  )
```

### Verdict

The knowledge graph is **well-connected**. The repository's cross-reference
network is healthier than initial estimates suggested, with only
`r findings$summary$broken_refs` broken references and
`r findings$summary$orphan_nodes` orphan nodes across
`r findings$registry$skills + findings$registry$agents + findings$registry$teams + findings$registry$guides`
registered items.

## Methodology

### Team Composition

The audit was conducted by an **opaque team** — a variable-size team of
shapeshifter agents that self-organized into the following roles:

```{r team-table}
team_df <- data.frame(
  Member = c(
    "Shapeshifter #1",
    "Shapeshifter #2",
    "Shapeshifter #3",
    "Shapeshifter #4"
  ),
  `Emerged Role` = c(
    "Coordinator / Integrator",
    "Reference Scanner",
    "Orphan Node Detective",
    "Integrity & Reciprocity Auditor"
  ),
  Scope = c(
    "Task decomposition, role assignment, report synthesis",
    "Broken cross-references across all .md files",
    "Registered items never referenced elsewhere",
    "Registry counts, non-reciprocal links"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(team_df) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### Coordination Pattern

**Adaptive** — roles emerged from task analysis, not pre-assignment.
The team followed four phases:

1. **Form Assessment**: Lead analyzed the audit task and decomposed it
2. **Role Assignment**: Lead assigned roles based on subtask requirements
3. **Execution**: Members worked in parallel on their assigned domains
4. **Integration**: Lead synthesized findings into this unified report

A **scope change** was injected mid-execution (Section 6: Self-Referential
Consistency), which the lead absorbed without disrupting ongoing work.

### Audit Scope

```{r scope-table}
scope_df <- data.frame(
  Section = 1:6,
  Domain = c(
    "Broken References",
    "Orphan Nodes",
    "Non-Reciprocal Cross-References",
    "Registry Integrity",
    "Remediation Plan",
    "Self-Referential Consistency"
  ),
  Description = c(
    "Internal links pointing to non-existent files",
    "Registered items never referenced by other files",
    "A references B but B does not reference A",
    "Registry counts vs files on disk",
    "Prioritized fixes by severity",
    "Opaque team auditing its own definition (added mid-execution)"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(scope_df) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## Section 1: Broken References {#sec-broken}

Broken references are internal cross-references (relative markdown links,
skill mentions in agent frontmatter, team member references) that point
to files or entries that do not exist on disk.

```{r broken-refs-table}
if (length(findings$broken_refs) > 0) {
  broken_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(findings$broken_refs, function(item) {
    data.frame(
      Source = item$source,
      Target = item$target,
      Type = item$type,
      Issue = item$issue,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))

  kable(broken_df) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
      full_width = TRUE,
      font_size = 13
    )
} else {
  cat("No broken references found.")
}
```

## Section 2: Orphan Nodes {#sec-orphans}

Orphan nodes are items registered in their `_registry.yml` but never
referenced by any other content file — no guide mentions them, no agent
lists them, no team includes them, and no skill cross-references them.

```{r orphan-summary}
orphan_summary <- data.frame(
  Category = c("Skills", "Agents", "Teams"),
  Registered = c(
    findings$registry$skills,
    findings$registry$agents,
    findings$registry$teams
  ),
  Orphaned = c(
    length(findings$orphans$skills),
    length(findings$orphans$agents),
    length(findings$orphans$teams)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
orphan_summary$`Connected (%)` <- round(
  (1 - orphan_summary$Orphaned / orphan_summary$Registered) * 100, 1
)

kable(orphan_summary, align = c("l", "r", "r", "r")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### Orphan Skills

```{r orphan-skills}
if (length(findings$orphans$skills) > 0) {
  skill_orphans <- data.frame(
    `Skill ID` = unlist(findings$orphans$skills),
    check.names = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  kable(skill_orphans) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
      full_width = FALSE
    )
} else {
  cat("No orphan skills found.")
}
```

### Orphan Agents

```{r orphan-agents}
if (length(findings$orphans$agents) > 0) {
  agent_orphans <- data.frame(
    `Agent ID` = unlist(findings$orphans$agents),
    check.names = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  kable(agent_orphans) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
      full_width = FALSE
    )
} else {
  cat("No orphan agents found.")
}
```

## Section 3: Non-Reciprocal Cross-References {#sec-reciprocal}

Non-reciprocal references occur when file A references file B in a context
where reciprocity is expected (Related Skills, See Also), but file B does
not reference A back.

```{r reciprocal-summary}
recip_summary <- data.frame(
  Context = c(
    "Skill Related Skills",
    "Agent See Also",
    "Team See Also"
  ),
  `Non-Reciprocal Pairs` = c(
    findings$non_reciprocal$skill_related,
    findings$non_reciprocal$agent_see_also,
    findings$non_reciprocal$team_see_also
  ),
  check.names = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(recip_summary, align = c("l", "r")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### Representative Examples

```{r reciprocal-examples}
if (length(findings$non_reciprocal$examples) > 0) {
  examples_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(
    findings$non_reciprocal$examples,
    function(item) {
      data.frame(
        From = item$from,
        References = item$references,
        `Back-reference` = item$back_ref,
        check.names = FALSE,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  ))

  kable(examples_df) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
      full_width = TRUE,
      font_size = 13
    )
} else {
  cat("No examples available.")
}
```

## Section 4: Registry Integrity {#sec-registry}

Registry integrity verifies that every file on disk has a matching registry
entry and every registry entry has a matching file on disk.

```{r registry-table}
reg_df <- data.frame(
  Registry = c(
    "skills/_registry.yml",
    "agents/_registry.yml",
    "teams/_registry.yml",
    "guides/_registry.yml"
  ),
  `Declared Count` = c(
    findings$registry$skills,
    findings$registry$agents,
    findings$registry$teams,
    findings$registry$guides
  ),
  `Files on Disk` = c(
    findings$registry$skills_disk,
    findings$registry$agents_disk,
    findings$registry$teams_disk,
    findings$registry$guides_disk
  ),
  Status = c(
    findings$registry$skills_status,
    findings$registry$agents_status,
    findings$registry$teams_status,
    findings$registry$guides_status
  ),
  check.names = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(reg_df, align = c("l", "r", "r", "c")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(4, color = ifelse(reg_df$Status == "PASS", "#2e7d32", "#c62828"))
```

## Section 5: Remediation Plan {#sec-remediation}

Findings are prioritized by severity. Broken references should be fixed
first (they cause navigation failures), followed by orphan nodes
(discoverability), then non-reciprocal references (completeness).

```{r remediation-table}
if (length(findings$remediation) > 0) {
  remed_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(findings$remediation, function(item) {
    data.frame(
      Priority = item$priority,
      Category = item$category,
      Action = item$action,
      Effort = item$effort,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))

  kable(remed_df) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
      full_width = TRUE
    ) |>
    row_spec(
      which(remed_df$Priority == "P1"),
      background = "#fce4ec"
    ) |>
    row_spec(
      which(remed_df$Priority == "P2"),
      background = "#fff8e1"
    )
} else {
  cat("No remediation items.")
}
```

## Section 6: Self-Referential Consistency {#sec-self-ref}

This section was added mid-execution as a scope change. The opaque team
audited its own definition files for internal consistency.

```{r self-ref-table}
self_ref <- findings$self_referential

self_df <- data.frame(
  Check = c(
    "Shapeshifter skills exist on disk",
    "Opaque team members match registry",
    "Shapeshifter See Also targets valid",
    "Opaque team See Also targets valid"
  ),
  Result = c(
    self_ref$shapeshifter_skills,
    self_ref$opaque_members,
    self_ref$shapeshifter_see_also,
    self_ref$opaque_see_also
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(self_df) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(
    2,
    color = ifelse(self_df$Result == "PASS", "#2e7d32", "#c62828")
  )
```

## Appendix A: Test Execution Metadata

```{r metadata-table}
meta_df <- data.frame(
  Field = c(
    "Test Scenario",
    "Target Team",
    "Coordination Pattern",
    "Team Size",
    "Acceptance Criteria Met",
    "Rubric Score",
    "Duration",
    "Scope Change"
  ),
  Value = c(
    "test-opaque-team-cartographers-audit",
    "opaque-team (4 shapeshifters)",
    "Adaptive",
    "4",
    paste0(findings$test_meta$criteria_passed, "/",
           findings$test_meta$criteria_total),
    paste0(findings$test_meta$rubric_score, "/",
           findings$test_meta$rubric_total),
    findings$test_meta$duration,
    "Section 6 added mid-execution"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(meta_df) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

*Generated from test run `2026-03-06-opaque-001`. Source:
[tests/results/2026-03-06-opaque-001/](https://github.com/pjt222/agent-almanac/tree/main/tests/results/2026-03-06-opaque-001/)*
